title: Aligning the Pixels
concept: RoIAlign replaces RoIPool in Mask R-CNN, using bilinear interpolation to extract feature maps without spatial quantization offsets.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What are the three parallel outputs of Mask R-CNN?

options.text: Class labels, bounding boxes, and pixel-level masks
Class labels, feature maps, and image resolutions
Bounding boxes, strides, and pool dimensions

exercises.explain: Correct! Mask R-CNN predicts classes, boxes, and masks in parallel.

cards.title: The misalignment problem
cards.body: In Faster R-CNN, RoIPool crops features of different sizes and forces them into a fixed grid (like 7x7) by rounding coordinates. Rounding (quantization) is fine for classification. But if a coordinate is rounded from 15.3 to 15, the mask shifts by 0.3 pixels. In deep features, a 0.3 pixel shift can mean a 10-pixel error on the original image! How do we fix this misalignment?

cards.title: RoIAlign
cards.body: RoIAlign is a feature extraction layer that avoids quantization. Instead of rounding coordinates, it uses **bilinear interpolation** to calculate the exact feature values at continuous coordinates, aligning features with the input pixels.

cards.title: Drawing on graph paper
cards.body: RoIPool is like drawing a circle on graph paper but only being allowed to color entire grid squares — the edges get blocky. RoIAlign is like using a ruler to draw the circle smoothly across the grid lines, calculating the exact fractions.

cards.title: Quantization offset
cards.body: If an RoI box has a width of 15.3 pixels, RoIPool rounds it to 15, introducing a spatial offset. RoIAlign splits the box into equal continuous sub-regions and samples values using math, preserving the exact 15.3 width.

cards.title: It matters for masks, not boxes
cards.body: You might think RoIAlign is needed for bounding boxes. Actually, a small coordinate shift doesn't affect box classification much. But it completely ruins segmentation masks, which require pixel-perfect alignment to trace contours.

cards.title: RoIPool vs. RoIAlign
cards.body: How coordinates are mapped to grids:

left.text:
Rounds coordinates (e.g., 2.7 -> 3)
Creates spatial misalignment
Blurry or shifted masks

right.text:
Uses bilinear interpolation (keeps 2.7)
Zero rounding error
Pixel-perfect mask alignment

exercises.prompt: What is the primary difference between RoIAlign and RoIPool?

options.text: RoIAlign uses bilinear interpolation to avoid coordinate rounding errors
RoIAlign flattens features into dense vectors
RoIAlign is designed only for CPU architectures
RoIAlign downsamples features using max pooling

exercises.explain: Correct! RoIAlign preserves continuous coordinates without rounding.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Coordinate rounding in RoIPool is called spatial quantization.
exercises.explain: True. Quantization refers to mapping continuous values to discrete integers.

exercises.sentence: RoIAlign uses bilinear ___ to calculate feature values at continuous points.
exercises.explain: Bilinear interpolation allows sampling features at fractional locations.

exercises.prompt: Why is RoIAlign crucial for Mask R-CNN?

options.text: Because segmentation masks require pixel-level alignment to be accurate
Because it reduces the file size of the model
Because it generates the initial region proposals

exercises.explain: Pixel-level precision is mandatory for high-quality segmentation masks.

exercises.prompt: You are training Mask R-CNN. The bounding boxes are highly accurate, but the masks look slightly shifted and do not line up with the object borders. What is the likely cause?

options.text: The architecture is using RoIPool instead of RoIAlign, causing quantization offsets that misalign the masks.
The learning rate is set too low for the mask branch.
The input images are stored in a compressed format.
The model has too many skip connections in the encoder.

exercises.explain: Perfect! Those pixel-level shifts are the classic symptom of RoIPool's rounding errors. Switching to RoIAlign solves this immediately. Next: how does the mask branch output masks?
